notes: This manifest reflects permissions used by shipped code today.
Future roadmap capabilities are tracked separately so the required list stays accurate.
Microsoft Defender scopes are external provider scopes and are not Microsoft Graph application permissions.
A small number of UI prompts still need least-privilege validation before they should be treated as final.

permissions.name: Application.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read app registrations and enterprise application objects.
permissions.why: Required for application inventory and service principal inventory views.
notes: This is missing from the current replit permission list.

permissions.name: Organization.Read.All
permissions.why: Used in overview and identity summary views.

permissions.name: Directory.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read directory objects such as users, groups, apps, and service principals.
permissions.why: Used across identity and app inventory routes.

permissions.name: Reports.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read Microsoft 365 usage and registration reports.
permissions.why: Used for user adoption, workload usage, and MFA registration reporting.
notes: This covers the current SharePoint usage reports, which is why Sites.Read.All is not required today.

permissions.name: Policy.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read tenant policies such as Conditional Access and authorization policy.
permissions.why: Used for Conditional Access reporting and app registration policy checks.

permissions.name: SecurityEvents.Read.All
permissions.why: Used for Secure Score cards and security posture views.

permissions.name: ServiceHealth.Read.All
permissions.why: Used for service announcement and health status views.

permissions.name: Team.ReadBasic.All
permissions.why: Used for Teams inventory and collaboration views.

permissions.name: UserAuthenticationMethod.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read tenant-wide user authentication method registration detail.
permissions.why: Used for MFA registration and passwordless capability reporting.

permissions.name: AuditLog.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read audit and compliance-related activity data.
permissions.why: Enhances compliance and enterprise app evidence without being required for the whole dashboard to load.
notes: The Service Principals screen treats this as optional for sign-in and enrichment data.

permissions.name: IdentityRiskEvent.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read identity protection risk events and risky users.
permissions.why: Current security route catches failures and degrades to partial risk reporting.
notes: The code currently uses this permission for both risk detections and risky users.

permissions.name: DeviceManagementManagedDevices.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read Intune managed device inventory and per-device details.
permissions.why: Enables full Intune device drill-down; current UI degrades when this is absent.
notes: This is required for per-device detail and discovered-app evidence in the current implementation.

permissions.name: DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read Intune compliance policies and configuration profiles.
permissions.why: Used for Intune policy and configuration summaries.

permissions.name: DeviceManagementApps.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read Intune application deployment and install state information.
permissions.why: Used only for the Intune app installation health section.

permissions.name: InformationProtectionPolicy.Read.All
permissions.summary: Read sensitivity labels and related information protection policy metadata.
permissions.why: Least-privilege application permission for listing sensitivity labels via Graph security informationProtection endpoints.
notes: Validated against Microsoft Graph permissions table for List sensitivityLabels (beta).

permissions.name: https://api.security.microsoft.com/.default
permissions.summary: Acquire a Microsoft Defender token for machine inventory.
permissions.why: Used by the security route for Defender machine data and must be documented separately from Graph permissions.
notes: The code also attempts the legacy alias https://api.securitycenter.microsoft.com/.default.

permissions.name: Sites.Read.All
permissions.why: This appears in older docs but is not required by the shipped code today.
notes: Keep this in the future tier until the product introduces direct SharePoint site API calls.

permissions.name: Exchange.ManageAsApp
permissions.summary: Potential future Exchange Online workload access outside Graph.
permissions.why: The roadmap calls out Exchange evidence that Graph alone cannot provide.
endpoints: Exchange Online PowerShell and workload-specific APIs
notes: This is future-facing and not consumed by the shipped code today.

features.title: Enterprise Application Registrations
notes: The current UI previously overstated Policy.Read.All as mandatory; the route hard-fails on applications read, not authorization policy read.

features.title: Service Principals (Enterprise Apps)
notes: Audit log access is treated as optional enrichment for sign-in and activity context.

features.title: Intune Devices and Compliance
notes: Per-device detail is the current gating factor; configuration data enriches the tab when available.

features.title: Compliance Sensitivity Labels
notes: Optional enrichment for sensitivity-label inventory in the compliance tab.
